slice.title: Concrete durable SagaStorePort instantiated and wired through engine, service main.ts, and standalone runner (G1)

gaps.title: No production SagaStorePort; runtime durability is memory-only
evidence: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/ports/saga-store-port.ts:23 — SagaStorePort is the persistent state store boundary for T1 saga runtime guarantees
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:67,77 — #store is optional (SagaStorePort | undefined)

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:277 — `if (!this.#store) return;` makes persistence a no-op when no store is injected; load/findByCorrelation/save/saveCorrelation/appendTransition (210,259,296,299,304) all guard on #store
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:90-93 — engine store set only if caller passes options.native.store; default native bus is store-less
plugins/sagas/src/runtime/saga-supervisor.ts:62-69 and saga-runner.ts:62-69 — deployed composition root never injects a store; runtimeOptions passed through with no store
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/stores/mod.ts — store subpath barrel re-exports only the SagaStorePort TYPE; contains no concrete store

grep 'implements SagaStorePort' across repo returns only MemorySagaStore (src/testing), RecordingSagaStore (src/testing), AbstractSagaStore (abstract), and a test-local class in tests/runtime/saga-store_test.ts
PrismaSagaStore appears only as comments in plugins/sagas/database/sagas.prisma referencing the EXTERNAL @saga-bus/store-prisma package, not a NetScript impl
KvSagaStore/PostgresSagaStore appear only in .llm/harness/profiles/sagas/extension-axes.md marked '(planned)' and architecture.md example prose — not implemented

gaps.title: Native engine constructed store-less by default → state silently non-durable
evidence: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:77 — this.#store = options.store (optional, no default durable store)

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:210 — const loaded = await this.#store?.load(instanceId) (optional-chained; store-less => always undefined => every handler starts from initialState)
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:277 — #persistTransition early-returns `if (!this.#store) return;` so save/saveCorrelation/appendTransition are silently skipped

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:90-93 — createNativeBus only sets store from options.store ?? options.engineOptions?.store; no default durable store injected and no warning logged
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/presets/start-sagas.ts:47-51 — startSagas('native') forwards options.native unchanged; default invocation has no store
repo-wide: only concrete SagaStorePort impls are test-only (src/testing/memory-saga-store.ts, recording-saga-store.ts); stores/mod.ts re-exports types only

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/logger.ts:8 — a warn() logger facility exists but is never invoked on the store-less composition path (create-saga-runtime.ts / saga-bus-bridge.ts contain no logger usage)

gaps.title: Deployed sagas service builds the runtime store-less, so saga state is fully ephemeral
evidence: plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — onStartup calls createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) with no native.store; this is the actual deployed entrypoint, registered at plugins/sagas/src/public/mod.ts:136 (.withService entrypoint './services/src/main.ts') and as deno.json start/dev/./services entry

plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:41 — dbClient = await ctx.db.getClient() is obtained but only passed to .withDatabase(dbClient) and startSagasStreamMirror; it is never threaded into createSagaRuntime, so the saga engine has no store

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:89-93 — createNativeBus builds the engine with store: options.store ?? options.engineOptions?.store; with no native options both are undefined, so SagaEngine.#store is undefined

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:67,277 — #store is optional; #persistTransition returns immediately when !this.#store, making save/saveCorrelation/appendTransition (lines 296-313) no-ops, and #store?.load (210) / #store?.findByCorrelation (259) yield undefined, so every message restarts from initialState

Repo-wide: only SagaStorePort impls are AbstractSagaStore (abstract) and MemorySagaStore/RecordingSagaStore exported only from packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/testing/mod.ts (lines 56-57) plus a test-local class — no production durable adapter
plugins/sagas/src/public/mod.ts:128 manifest withDescription 'Durable saga orchestration...' and database/sagas.prisma schema (mod.ts:139) advertise durability that the deployed runtime never wires

gaps.title: Saga durability promised in docs but no concrete store exists and the service wires the engine store-less
evidence: plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) called with no native.store; the live service runs the native engine store-less
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:89-93 — createNativeBus builds createSagaEngine with store: options.store ?? options.engineOptions?.store (both undefined here)

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:277 — #persistTransition early-returns `if (!this.#store) return;` so no state is ever persisted; lines 210/259 #store?.load and #store?.findByCorrelation return undefined => no recovery across restart

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/abstracts/abstract-saga-store.ts:16, src/testing/memory-saga-store.ts:17, src/testing/recording-saga-store.ts:25 — the ONLY SagaStorePort implementors; all stub/test-only, no durable KV/Prisma/Postgres production adapter in packages/ or plugins/

plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:12 — docstring claims 'Prisma store for durable saga state'; plugins/sagas/database/sagas.prisma references @saga-bus/store-prisma PrismaSagaStore, which is a third-party package wired only into the DEPRECATED legacy adapter (packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-legacy.ts wrapping @saga-bus/core), not the native engine

Note: cited docs-site files docs/site/reference/sagas/index.md and docs/site/explanation/plugin-model.md do not exist in this worktree (docs live on a separate branch); those specific taglines were not verifiable here

gaps.title: Sagas service composition root builds the saga engine with no SagaStorePort — durability silently inert
evidence: plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — sagaRuntime = createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }); no store/native.store passed, so the deployed service engine runs storeless

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:89-93 — createNativeBus uses options.store ?? options.engineOptions?.store; when undefined the engine is constructed with store === undefined

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:210,259,277-313 — engine IS store-aware: #handleEntry calls #store?.load, #resolveInstanceId calls #store?.findByCorrelation, #persistTransition calls store.save/saveCorrelation/appendTransition; with store undefined load returns undefined and base state resets every message, so instance state is transient per-process and lost on restart

No production SagaStorePort impl in repo — only test/abstract: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/testing/memory-saga-store.ts:17, src/testing/recording-saga-store.ts:25, src/abstracts/abstract-saga-store.ts:16, tests/runtime/saga-store_test.ts:128

.llm/harness/profiles/sagas/extension-axes.md:15 — KvSagaStore/PostgresSagaStore explicitly '(planned)'; architecture.md:103 createKvSagaStore is illustrative and not implemented; sagas.prisma references external @saga-bus/store-prisma not present in repo
plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:10,12 — public docstring promises 'SSE streaming for real-time saga updates' and 'Prisma store for durable saga state', neither wired in the composition root

gaps.title: Sagas service fetches dbClient and binds it to HTTP routers but never passes it to the saga runtime
evidence: plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:41 — const dbClient = await ctx.db.getClient(); resolved once
plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:56 — .withDatabase(dbClient) binds dbClient to the HTTP/router layer only

plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) is called with NO native.store; CreateSagaRuntimeOptions.native.store (create-saga-runtime.ts:30) is the only way to inject a store and it is omitted
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:89-93 — createNativeBus passes options.store (undefined) into createSagaEngine; engine store is optional with no default

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:67,77,277 — #store is optional; persist path is `if (!this.#store) return;` so with no store all engine writes (save/saveCorrelation/appendTransition) are silent no-ops

plugins/sagas/services/src/routers/v1-handlers.ts:65,71,114,191 — listInstances/getInstance/getInstanceHistory read directly from db.sagaInstance / db.sagaExecutionHistory Prisma tables, which the engine never writes
plugins/sagas/streams/producer.ts:36-51,95-111 — startSagasStreamMirror only READS sagaInstance (findMany) and mirrors into a durable stream; it never writes SagaInstance rows
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/stores/mod.ts — ./stores exports only the SagaStorePort type; no concrete persistent (Prisma) store exists in the repo

gaps.title: Saga Postgres schema declares it is the required schema for @saga-bus/store-prisma PrismaSagaStore, but PrismaSagaStore is never imported or constructed anywhere
evidence: plugins/sagas/database/sagas.prisma:3,10 — comments promise Postgres durability via PrismaSagaStore and call it 'the required schema for @saga-bus/store-prisma PrismaSagaStore'.
Repo-wide grep for 'PrismaSagaStore|store-prisma|@saga-bus/store-prisma' matches ONLY sagas.prisma comments. deno.json declares only '@saga-bus/core': '^0.2.2' — store-prisma is not a dependency.

plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — composition root constructs the engine as createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) with NO store argument; SagaEngine.#store stays undefined (packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:54,67,77).
Only SagaStorePort implementations in the repo are test-only: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/testing/memory-saga-store.ts and recording-saga-store.ts. No production Prisma store.

Write path is dead: no runtime code writes to saga_instances. The only sagaInstance.create reference is a doc-comment example at packages/database/extensions/sql-json.extension.ts:520. (Read paths exist: services/src/routers/v1-handlers.ts:62-71,114 and streams/producer.ts:95 query db.sagaInstance — so the table is migrated and read but never populated.)

gaps.title: Background saga runner / supervisor also defaults runtimeOptions to {} so the standalone saga process runs store-less too
evidence: plugins/sagas/src/runtime/saga-runner.ts:27 — runtimeOptions type allows omit; saga-runner.ts:62-69 passes { ...options.runtimeOptions, adapter } so native.store is never set
plugins/sagas/src/runtime/saga-supervisor.ts:66-68 — factory called with this.options.runtimeOptions ?? {}; saga-supervisor.ts:121-125 createDefaultRuntime forwards verbatim, nothing injects a store
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:89-93 — createNativeBus({}) builds engine with store: undefined
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:54,67,77 — #store is optional; undefined store runs non-durable with no error
plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — dependsOn HTTP service also store-less: createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) with no native.store despite 'Prisma store for durable saga state' header

No concrete production SagaStorePort impl in repo: only test-only MemorySagaStore/RecordingSagaStore under packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/testing/; extension-axes docs mark PostgresSagaStore '(planned)'

slice.title: Saga idempotencyKey end-to-end: HTTP contract, durable SagaIdempotencyPort, engine applied-key reservation

gaps.title: Idempotency dedup defaults to in-memory; no durable SagaIdempotencyPort impl
evidence: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-idempotency.ts:33 — SagaIdempotencyDedupTable doc: in-memory for local dev/tests, 'use a durable port in production'
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-idempotency.ts:96 — MemorySagaIdempotencyStore is the only first-party SagaIdempotencyPort impl (process-memory only)
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:180-182 — toIdempotencyPort() defaults to new MemorySagaIdempotencyStore() when none supplied
Repo-wide grep 'implements SagaIdempotencyPort': only MemorySagaIdempotencyStore + test RecordingIdempotencyStore; no Kv/Redis durable dedup anywhere

Contrast: triggers-core ships durable KvTriggerIdempotencyStore (plugins/triggers/src/runtime/kv-trigger-runtime-stores.ts:104) and defaults to it — the durable idempotency pattern exists in the repo but was never built for sagas

gaps.title: Deployed service falls back to in-memory idempotency, so dedup is lost on restart and not shared across replicas
evidence: plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) supplies no native.idempotency, the deployed entrypoint (import.meta.main, line 88-91)
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:89-101 — createNativeBus forwards undefined idempotency to the bridge when native options omit it
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:177-187 — toIdempotencyPort returns new MemorySagaIdempotencyStore() when none provided
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-idempotency.ts:33-37,96-110 — MemorySagaIdempotencyStore is backed by a process-local Map and is doc-flagged 'use a durable port in production'
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:74-97,137-139 — publish/dispatchCascaded dedup relies entirely on the in-memory port
plugins/triggers/src/runtime/kv-trigger-runtime-stores.ts:104 — KvTriggerIdempotencyStore shows a durable KV-backed idempotency port exists for triggers but has no saga equivalent

gaps.title: Sagas HTTP publish endpoint drops the inbound idempotencyKey, defeating the saga bus dedup on every client retry
evidence: plugins/sagas/contracts/v1/sagas.contract.ts:314-319 — PublishMessageInputZodSchema defines only {type, payload, correlationId, topic}; bound to POST /publish at line 437-439, so oRPC strips inbound idempotencyKey before the handler.

plugins/sagas/services/src/routers/v1-handlers.ts:213-226 — publishSagaMessage destructures only {type, payload, correlationId} and calls runtime.publish(message, {traceparent, tracestate}); no idempotencyKey on message or options.
plugins/sagas/src/runtime/saga-publisher.ts:179 — createPublishHttpInput sends idempotencyKey in the body, so the client key reaches the endpoint and is then discarded.
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:75-81 — publish() only reserves/dedups when idempotencyKey is present; with the key dropped, dedup is bypassed.
grep of plugins/sagas: idempotencyKey appears only in saga-publisher.ts (sender) and docs/http-publisher.md — absent from all receiving services/routers/contracts files.

gaps.title: Saga idempotency defaults to process-memory store, so dedup state is lost on restart/crash and not shared across instances
evidence: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:177-187 — toIdempotencyPort() returns `new MemorySagaIdempotencyStore()` when idempotency arg is undefined (verified).

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-idempotency.ts:33-126 — SagaIdempotencyDedupTable is a private Map; MemorySagaIdempotencyStore wraps it; JSDoc says 'use a durable port in production' (verified).
plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:63 — createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) supplies no idempotency/store; native bus falls back to memory dedup (verified).

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:32,100 — SagaRuntimeNativeOptions.idempotency exists and is forwarded to the bridge, but main.ts never sets it (verified path is wired but unused).

grep 'implements SagaIdempotencyPort' → only MemorySagaIdempotencyStore (prod) and RecordingIdempotencyStore (test); KvTriggerIdempotencyStore exists for triggers (plugins/triggers/src/runtime/kv-trigger-runtime-stores.ts) but has no saga equivalent (verified).

gaps.title: Saga engine re-runs handlers and re-persists transitions on retry without an applied-key guard at the engine boundary
evidence: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:103-105 — SagaEngine.publish() calls handle(message) with no dedup; engine holds no SagaIdempotencyPort (only #store: SagaStorePort at line 67).

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:142-157,197-251 — handle()/#handleEntry() always execute the handler and call #persistTransition(); `attempt` only flows into SagaContext (line 220), never an applied-key/version guard.
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:115-121 — dispatchCascaded forwards idempotencyKey into publish(), but publish()/handle() never read it.
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/mod.ts:63 — `createSagaEngine` and `SagaEngine` are public exports, so consumers can drive the raw engine directly and bypass the bridge reservation.

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:44,53,74-84,137-139 — the only idempotency reserve() lives in SagaBusBridge above the engine; create-saga-runtime.ts:89-102 wires it but the engine layer remains unguarded.

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-idempotency.ts:51-70,129-134 — bridge dedup is an in-memory TTL reservation keyed on (message.type, idempotencyKey), first-wins, NOT a durable applied-key check against persisted transitions, so it does not satisfy the doctrine guard even when used.

docs/architecture/doctrine/08-runtime-state-failure.md:202-221 — exactly-once-effective: the store records applied keys and rejects duplicates with a structured already-applied outcome (doctrine quote matches verbatim; filename is 08-runtime-state-failure.md).

slice.title: Saga telemetry seam + handle-path spans + API publish trace linkage

gaps.title: createSagaRuntime / SagaEngineOptions expose no tracer/instrumentation injection point
evidence: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:51-55 — SagaEngineOptions = { id, defaultRetryPolicy, store }; #handleEntry (197-252) emits no telemetry span
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:24-40,89-102 — runtime options + createNativeBus expose/thread no tracer
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:27-34 — SagaBusBridgeOptions has no instrumentation field
plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:11,63 — header advertises 'OpenTelemetry tracing' but composition root calls createSagaRuntime({ adapter: 'native' }) with no tracer

packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/telemetry/instrumentation.ts:34-44,140-158 — SagaTelemetryTracer + SagaInstrumentation (NOOP default) exist but have zero call sites outside the telemetry dir (Grep across src/ and plugins/sagas returned no matches)

gaps.title: SagaEngine handle path emits no spans; trace context arrives as data and dead-ends
evidence: packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:1-22 — imports only domain + ports; no telemetry/tracer/SagaInstrumentation import
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:222-225 — traceparent/tracestate copied into SagaContext then handler invoked at 225 with no span created or activated
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:290-291 — trace context persisted into envelope metadata as data, never as an active span parent
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:51-55,74-78 — SagaEngineOptions/constructor expose no tracer or instrumentation seam
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/adapters/saga-bus-bridge.ts:189-197 — withPublishOptions forwards traceparent/tracestate as message fields with zero span creation
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/telemetry/instrumentation.ts:150-168 — SagaInstrumentation.startHandleSpan exists but grep shows it is unreferenced outside the telemetry module

grep across packages/plugin-sagas-core/src and plugins/sagas: SagaInstrumentation/startHandleSpan/startSpan/tracer match only telemetry/{instrumentation,mod,attributes}.ts; plugins/sagas has zero matches despite main.ts:11 advertising 'OpenTelemetry tracing'

gaps.title: Saga publish SSE event records trace headers as opaque data only; no span bridges API publish to engine handle
evidence: plugins/sagas/services/src/routers/v1-handlers.ts:130-136 — publish handler reads getTraceContext(), converts to plain headers, forwards to runtime.publish; never activates a span/context around the call
plugins/sagas/services/src/routers/v1-handlers.ts:228-233 — 'saga:message_received' SSE event embeds trace headers in event.data.headers as plain JSON (observability-as-data)
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-engine.ts:142-252 — SagaEngine.handle/#handleEntry create no span and hold no tracer; only read+persist traceparent/tracestate as data (lines 290-291)
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/create-saga-runtime.ts:89-102 — createNativeBus wires engine+bridge with no tracer/instrumentation; SagaEngineOptions/SagaBusBridgeOptions accept none
packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/telemetry/instrumentation.ts:140-168 — saga.handle span facade exists but defaults to NOOP_TRACER and is never invoked by the engine/bridge runtime path
plugins/sagas/services/src/main.ts:58,63 — .withRPC({traceContext:true}) establishes the server span, but the native runtime it constructs emits no child saga span

slice.title: Authentication + authorization (authn middleware + authz/RBAC) seam on @netscript/service

gaps.title: No authentication primitive anywhere in @netscript/service (only withCors)
evidence: packages/service/mod.ts:66-127 — public surface exports only health, openapi, handlers, types, createService, defineService; no auth/bearer/jwt/apikey/session export

packages/service/src/builder/service-builder.ts:59-122 — ServiceBuilder interface has withCors/withLogger/withDatabase/withHealthCheck/withReadinessCheck/withOpenAPI/withDocs/withRPC/withContext/onStartup/withHealth/use/route/withServiceInfo; no withAuth/withBearer/withApiKey/requireAuth
packages/service/src/builder/service-builder-impl.ts:73-76 — withCors (default origin:'*') is the only security-adjacent middleware; no auth middleware installed anywhere in the builder
packages/service/src/presets/define-service.ts:112-142 — Layer 3 defineService preset wires CORS/logger/OpenAPI/docs/RPC/serviceInfo/optional-DB but installs no authentication seam

package-wide search: only matches for auth/bearer/jwt/apikey in packages/service/src are DB connectivity 'authHint' strings (diagnostics/database-connectivity.ts); withAuth* hits elsewhere are the unrelated withAuthor plugin-metadata method

gaps.title: No authorization / RBAC / scope-enforcement layer in the service surface
evidence: packages/service/src/builder/service-builder.ts:59-122 — ServiceBuilder interface has no authorize/withScopes/requireRole method; only CORS/logger/db/health/OpenAPI/docs/RPC/context/startup/use/route/service-info
packages/service/src/types.ts:88-119 — ServiceContext exposes req, response helpers, and generic get(key) only; no principal/subject/claims/roles
packages/service/src/builder/service-builder-impl.ts:240-258 — buildRpcContext injects only db + trace headers into oRPC context; no auth principal
packages/service/src/presets/define-service.ts:112-143 — Layer-3 defineService preset wires no authn/authz
packages/telemetry/src/orpc/error-plugin.ts:172 — only 'UNAUTHORIZED' occurrence is an oRPC error-code enum string, not an enforcement control

slice.title: Signal handlers + onShutdown/drain hook on the service front door

gaps.title: Service front door never installs SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers; serve() runs until the process is killed
evidence: packages/service/src/builder/service-listener.ts:24-72 — startServiceListener() bridges only optional options.signal into an AbortController and exposes stop(); no Deno.addSignalListener for SIGTERM/SIGINT.
packages/service/src/builder/service-builder-impl.ts:397-407 — serve() runs startup hooks then startServiceListener(); no signal wiring or drain registration.
packages/service/src/presets/define-service.ts:142 — defineService() resolves to .withHealth().serve(); no signal wiring.

Repo-wide grep addSignalListener|SIGTERM|SIGINT|SIGBREAK matches only plugin background loops (plugins/triggers/src/runtime/trigger-processor.ts:69-70, plugins/sagas/src/runtime/saga-runner.ts:41-46,179) and plugins/streams/services/src/main.ts:153-154 — none in packages/service.
plugins/workers/services/src/main.ts:48-73 — deployed HTTP front door uses createService(...).serve() with no signal handlers, inheriting the gap.
plugins/streams/services/src/main.ts:141-154 — bypasses the service builder with raw Deno.serve and hand-rolls SIGINT/SIGTERM→frontServer.shutdown(), evidencing the builder's missing capability.

gaps.title: ServiceBuilder exposes onStartup() but no onShutdown()/onDrain() hook, so runtime resources are never torn down with the HTTP server
evidence: packages/service/src/builder/service-builder.ts:97 — ServiceBuilder interface declares onStartup(hook) only; no onShutdown/onStop/onDrain counterpart.

packages/service/src/builder/service-builder-impl.ts:58,299-302,397-407 — only startupHooks[] exists and runs before serve(); no shutdownHooks array, no teardown registration, serve() returns the listener handle directly.

packages/service/src/builder/service-listener.ts:62-71 — RunningService.stop() only aborts the HTTP AbortController and awaits server.finished; invokes no user-supplied teardown, so runtime resources (workers/sagas/DB) are never released.
packages/service/src/types.ts:43-50 — ServeOptions has only {port?, signal?}; no drainTimeoutMs/onShutdown.
packages/service/src/presets/define-service.ts:133 — Layer-3 preset wires only onStartup; no shutdown path even with a DB.

packages/plugin-workers-core/src/shutdown/shutdown-manager.ts + runtime/composition-root.ts:117-143 — a ShutdownManager exists but is plugin-internal and never wired into RunningService.stop() (orphaned from the HTTP lifecycle).
docs/architecture/doctrine/08-runtime-state-failure.md:133-134 — stop() must abort an internal controller and await drain; no service-surface seam satisfies this.

slice.title: Worker consumer-side applied-keys dedup + JobMessage idempotency propagation

gaps.title: Worker job/task consumers have no applied-keys dedup; at-least-once redelivery double-applies side effects
evidence: plugins/workers/worker/job-dispatcher.ts:31-119 — processWorkerJob unconditionally creates execution and runs executeWorkerJob with no applied-key check and no read of message attempts/deliveryCount
plugins/workers/worker/job-dispatcher.ts:140-170 — processWorkerTask unconditionally runs taskExecutor.execute with no dedup
plugins/workers/worker/worker.ts:251-267 — listenForJobs forwards every delivery into processWorkerJob with no dedup gate
packages/queue/adapters/kv-polling.adapter.ts:497-522 — nack() requeues SAME message id with incremented attempts (at-least-once redelivery)
packages/queue/adapters/kv-polling.adapter.ts:529-541 — recoverTimedOutMessages() re-nacks visibility-expired messages, redelivering them
packages/queue/adapters/kv-polling.adapter.ts:546-559 — MessageContext.deliveryCount = message.attempts exists but no worker code consumes it

packages/plugin-workers-core/src/runtime/runtime-types.ts — only correlationId present; no idempotencyKey/deduplicationId/applied-keys surface (grep across packages/plugin-workers-core for idempotency returns zero matches)
docs/architecture/doctrine/08-runtime-state-failure.md:202-221 — doctrine promises at-least-once with idempotency keys and a store that records applied keys and rejects duplicates

CONTRAST: packages/plugin-triggers-core/src/ports/trigger-idempotency-port.ts and packages/plugin-sagas-core/src/runtime/saga-idempotency.ts implement the applied-key pattern for sibling subsystems but are NOT consumed by the workers runtime

gaps.title: JobMessage carries no idempotency key into the worker, so the trigger's dedup intent is not propagated past the queue
evidence: packages/plugin-workers-core/src/runtime/runtime-types.ts:134-161 — JobMessage and TaskMessage define no named idempotencyKey/deduplicationId field; nothing populates an idempotency key on the message body.

plugins/triggers/src/runtime/trigger-runtime-processor.ts:111-119,142 — message built without an idempotency field; the key is used ONLY as queue deduplicationId at line 142, not stored on the message.
plugins/workers/worker/job-dispatcher.ts:22 — consumer destructures only {jobId, payload, correlationId}; processWorkerJob creates executionState with no idempotency key.
Grep idempotenc/dedup/appliedKeys across plugins/workers and packages/plugin-workers-core/src — zero matches; no effect-time applied-keys store exists in the worker path.
MITIGATION (does not resolve): packages/queue/adapters/kv-polling.adapter.ts:297-323 enforces deduplicationId via KV TTL marker at enqueue only.

doctrine docs/architecture/doctrine/08-runtime-state-failure.md:214-217 — requires every inbound message carry an idempotency key, handler effects keyed on it, and an applied-keys store rejecting duplicates with a structured 'already applied' outcome.

slice.title: Uniform dead-letter-queue contract across default/native adapters

gaps.title: No dead-letter queue on the default/native adapters (DenoKv, Postgres, Redis, AMQP); only KvPolling has a DLQ
evidence: packages/queue/adapters/kv-polling.adapter.ts:471-496 — only adapter with a real DLQ + max-attempts handling (plus reprocessDlq:726, dlq depth metrics:677-697)
packages/queue/adapters/postgres.adapter.ts:409-416 + 420-427 — nack({requeue:false}) calls this.ack() which is a hard DELETE; poison message silently discarded, no DLQ record
packages/queue/adapters/redis.adapter.ts:334-339 — nack({requeue:false}) does lrem from processing and pushes nowhere; message lost, no DLQ
packages/queue/adapters/deno-kv.adapter.ts:302-304 — ack/nack are async () => {} no-ops; no DLQ (and no real ack/redelivery) semantics
packages/queue/adapters/amqp.adapter.ts:218-219 — ack/nack are async () => {} no-ops

packages/queue/factory/create-queue.ts:124,149-162,214-243 — default/auto-discovery selects RabbitMQ/Redis/DenoKv/Postgres; DLQ-capable KvPolling reachable only when isKvConnect is true (create-queue.ts:256)
packages/queue/factory/create-typed-queue.ts:174-177 — onValidationError:'dlq' action only calls context.nack({requeue:false}), inheriting the per-adapter gap
packages/queue/ports/options.ts:224-229 + README.md:81,226 — advertise onValidationError:'dlq' as a supported dead-letter capability
Grep dlq/deadLetter across packages/queue — implementation matches only in kv-polling.adapter.ts
